# Assurance Ladder

Use this ladder to turn vague confidence into operational confidence.

## Level 0 — Folklore

```text
“We think this hook might work.”
```

No guarantee. No method. No metric.

## Level 1 — Named hypothesis

```text
H: Result-first first frames increase chose-to-view.
Metric: CTV.
Method: M-MPT.
```

Guarantee: the idea is testable.

## Level 2 — Preflight-pass asset

The video passes quality gates:

```text
first frame legible
hook specific
text readable
payoff before delay
CTA aligned
caption/title classify topic
```

Guarantee: the asset is not failing obvious quality controls before release.

## Level 3 — Published controlled test

At least two variants are published with one major variable changed.

Guarantee: the result is interpretable.

## Level 4 — First signal readout

After 24h/72h, the video is assigned to a gate:

```text
classification
scroll-stop
early hold
body retention
completion
save/send/comment/follow
search long-tail
trust
```

Guarantee: failure is diagnosed.

## Level 5 — Replication trio

The mechanism wins or holds up across three related videos.

Guarantee: the result is less likely to be luck.

## Level 6 — Platform/surface robustness

The mechanism survives adaptation to another platform or surface.

Guarantee: the mechanism is not purely platform-specific noise.

## Level 7 — Portfolio integration

The mechanism becomes part of a content portfolio:

```text
feed-native version
search-native version
sendable version
saveable version
series version
```

Guarantee: the mechanism has multiple distribution paths.

## Level 8 — Controlled exposure

The mechanism is seeded using paid/owned/partner/community distribution.

Guarantee: minimum data volume is obtained even if organic recommendation is weak.

## Level 9 — Operating standard

The mechanism becomes a named recurring format.

Guarantee: the system can repeatedly produce variants without reinventing strategy.
